perturbation.original_text: Except as expressly permitted herein, for a period of thr ee years from the effective da te (Non-Disclosure Period), Recipient shall maintain in confidence and not disclose Conf idential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as expressly permitted herein, for a period of one year from the effective date (Non-Disclosure Period), Recipient shall maintain in confidence and not disclose Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the non-disclosure period from three years to one year may be insufficient to protect certain trade secrets under Indiana law, creating a legal contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Indiana Uniform Trade Secrets Act, Ind. Code § 24-2-3-1 et seq.
perturbation.law_explanation: Indiana's Uniform Trade Secrets Act defines misappropriation and provides legal recourse for trade secret violations. Reducing the confidentiality period to one year may not provide adequate protection or remedies, especially if the useful life of the trade secret is longer, thereby potentially conflicting with the Act's intent to protect trade secrets for a reasonable duration.

Definitions :: 2023 Indiana Code :: U.S. Codes and Statutes :: U.S. Law :: Justia There Is a Newer Version of this Section 2024 Indiana Code 2023 Indiana Code (here) 2022 Indiana Code View All Versions View Our Newest Version Here 2023 Indiana Code Title 24. Trade Regulation Article 2. Trademarks, Trade Names, and Trade Secrets Chapter 3. Trade Secrets 24-2-3-2. Definitions Universal Citation: IN Code § 24-2-3-2 (2023) Learn more This media-neutral citation is based on the American Association of Law Libraries Universal Citation Guide and is not necessarily the official citation. Previous Next Sec. 2. As used in this chapter, unless the context requires otherwise: "Improper means" includes theft, bribery, misrepresentation, breach or inducement of a breach of a duty to maintain secrecy, or espionage through electronic or other means. "Misappropriation" means: (1) acquisition of a trade secret of another by a person who knows or has reason to know that the trade secret was acquired by improper means; or (2) disclosure or use of a trade secret of another without express or implied consent by a person who: (A) used improper means to acquire knowledge of the trade secret; (B) at the time of disclosure or use, knew or had reason to know that his knowledge of the trade secret was: (i) derived from or through a person who had utilized improper means to acquire it; (ii) acquired under circumstances giving rise to a duty to maintain its secrecy or limit its use; or (iii) derived from or through a person who owed a duty to the person seeking relief to maintain its secrecy or limit its use; or (C) before a material change of his position, knew or had reason to know that it was a trade secret and that knowledge of it had been acquired by accident or mistake. "Person" means a natural person, limited liability company, corporation, business trust, estate, trust, partnership, association, joint venture, government, governmental subdivision or agency, or any other legal or
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the non-disclosure period from three years to one year, which may be insufficient to protect trade secrets under Indiana law. The Law Explanation states that a shorter period might not provide adequate protection if the trade secret's useful life is longer, potentially conflicting with the intent of the Indiana Uniform Trade Secrets Act. Scraped Law Snippet 2, Indiana Code § 24-2-3-2, defines 'misappropriation' and outlines duties to maintain secrecy. Reducing the protection period weakens the ability to prevent misappropriation, especially if the trade secret retains value beyond one year, thus contradicting the purpose of trade secret protection.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that Recipient is required by the Indiana Open Records Act, or any other law, to disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information, Recipient shall promptly notify Discloser, consult with Discloser re garding whether there are legitimate grounds to narrow or contest such disclosure, and only disclose that information that the University, in the opinion of legal counsel, is legally obligated to disclose.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that Recipient is required by the Indiana Open Records Act, or any other law, to disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information, Recipient is authorized to disclose the information without any prior notification to Discloser.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the requirement for the Recipient to notify and consult with the Discloser before disclosing confidential information under the Indiana Open Records Act conflicts with the Discloser's legal rights to protect legitimately confidential information and seek legal remedies to prevent unlawful disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Indiana Open Records Act, Ind. Code § 5-14-3-1 et seq.
perturbation.law_explanation: While the Indiana Open Records Act mandates the disclosure of public records, it also provides exemptions for certain confidential information. By authorizing disclosure without notification, the changed text prevents the Discloser from invoking these exemptions or seeking legal recourse to protect genuinely confidential information as permitted under the Act, potentially leading to unlawful disclosure.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Indiana General Assembly You need to enable JavaScript to run this app.
perturbation.justification: The modified contract text removes the Recipient's obligation to notify the Discloser before disclosing confidential information under the Indiana Open Records Act. The Law Explanation states this prevents the Discloser from protecting confidential information and seeking legal recourse, potentially leading to unlawful disclosure. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is incomplete, the Perturbation Explanation reinforces the contradiction by highlighting the infringement on the Discloser's legal rights to protect their confidential information. This directly contradicts the intent of the original text, which aimed to ensure compliance with the Open Records Act while safeguarding confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: Any cl aim, suit, or cause of action involving the interpretation, application, or enforcement of this Agreement shall be commenced in Delaware County Circuit Court in Muncie, Indiana.
perturbation.changed_text: Any claim, suit, or cause of action involving the interpretation, application, or enforcement of this Agreement shall be commenced in Marion County Small Claims Court in Indianapolis, Indiana.
perturbation.explanation: Specifying Marion County Small Claims Court may limit the jurisdiction and remedies available to the parties, potentially conflicting with Indiana law regarding jurisdiction based on the amount in controversy and the nature of the claim.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Indiana Rules of Trial Procedure, Rule 75
perturbation.law_citation: Indiana Rules of Trial Procedure, Rule 75
perturbation.law_explanation: Indiana Rules of Trial Procedure outline venue requirements. Small claims courts have jurisdictional limits (dollar amount). For disputes exceeding the jurisdictional limit, designating small claims court improperly restricts proper venue and remedies available, which is contrary to Indiana law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text specifies Marion County Small Claims Court, which inherently limits the jurisdictional limit based on the dollar amount of the dispute. The Law Explanation clarifies that designating a small claims court improperly restricts proper venue and remedies available for disputes exceeding that jurisdictional limit, thus creating a legal contradiction because it violates Indiana Rules of Trial Procedure regarding proper venue.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time, without cause, effective imme diately upon written notice of termin ation; however, in the event this Agreement is terminated prior to expiration of the Non-Disc losure Period, its provisions shall survive and remain in effect for the remainder of the Non-Disclosure Period, with respect to Confidential Information disclosed prior to the effective date of termination.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time, without cause, effective immediately upon verbal notice of termination; the provisions regarding confidentiality will not survive any termination.
perturbation.explanation: Requiring only verbal notice for termination and eliminating the survival clause for confidentiality obligations contradicts standard contract law principles and may fail to adequately protect trade secrets or confidential information under Indiana law.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Indiana Uniform Trade Secrets Act, Ind. Code § 24-2-3-1 et seq.
perturbation.law_explanation: Eliminating the survival clause and permitting verbal termination undermines the protection of trade secrets. The Indiana Uniform Trade Secrets Act offers remedies, including injunctions, to prevent misappropriation. By negating continued confidentiality, the changed text contradicts the Act's purpose of protecting valuable trade secrets after the agreement ends, as verbal notice provides no record for evidence.

Injunction Against Misappropriation; Exceptional Circumstances :: 2022 Indiana Code :: U.S. Codes and Statutes :: U.S. Law :: Justia There Is a Newer Version of this Section 2024 Indiana Code 2023 Indiana Code 2022 Indiana Code (here) View All Versions View Our Newest Version Here 2022 Indiana Code Title 24. Trade Regulation Article 2. Trademarks, Trade Names, and Trade Secrets Chapter 3. Trade Secrets 24-2-3-3. Injunction Against Misappropriation; Exceptional Circumstances Universal Citation: IN Code § 24-2-3-3 (2022) Learn more This media-neutral citation is based on the American Association of Law Libraries Universal Citation Guide and is not necessarily the official citation. Previous Next Sec. 3. (a) Actual or threatened misappropriation may be enjoined. Upon application to the court, an injunction shall be terminated when the trade secret has ceased to exist, but the injunction may be continued for an additional reasonable period of time in order to eliminate commercial advantage that otherwise would be derived from the misappropriation. (b) If the court determines in exceptional circumstances that it would be unreasonable to prohibit future use, an injunction may condition future use upon payment of a reasonable royalty for no longer than the period of time the use could have been prohibited. (c) In appropriate circumstances, affirmative acts to protect a trade secret may be compelled by court order. As added by Acts 1982, P.L.148, SEC.1. Amended by P.L.50-1984, SEC.3. perturbation.justification: The modified contract text eliminates the survival clause for confidentiality and changes the notice requirement to verbal. This directly contradicts the purpose of protecting trade secrets as outlined in the Indiana Uniform Trade Secrets Act, as explained in the Law Explanation. Scraped Law Snippet 2, Indiana Code § 24-2-3-3, allows for injunctions against misappropriation of trade secrets. By removing the survival clause, the changed text undermines the possibility of such injunctions after termination, particularly since verbal notice offers no documented proof. This change weakens the legal protection for trade secrets intended by the Act, thereby creating a significant contradiction.
